description: Harden Ralph backlog runtime with ordered task selection, Review status, script-side completion verification, and schema-validated verifier output.

userStories.title: Add Review status to backlog config
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need backlog status order to include `Review` so the queue can represent pre-verifier review explicitly and later runtime changes can rely on status order from `backlog/config.yml`.
acceptanceCriteria: `backlog/config.yml` statuses are exactly `["To Do", "In Progress", "Review", "Review Failed", "Done"]`
No unrelated backlog config values change
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: `backlog/config.yml` currently has `To Do`, `In Progress`, `Review Failed`, `Done` and no `Review` state. Target: add `Review` between `In Progress` and `Review Failed` because the approved runtime plan uses backlog status order as queue precedence. Files to inspect before editing: `backlog/config.yml`, `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` (Summary, Runtime policy and config, Interfaces), and `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Workflow Implementation Audit.md` for current-state drift. A dumb agent should treat this as a pure config change with no runtime code in scope.

userStories.title: Add Ralph runtime config file and load it
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need stable runtime policy in a root `config.yaml` so allowed assignees, review defaults, and fix-loop limits are static config instead of hardcoded shell behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Repo root contains `config.yaml` with keys `selection.allowed_assignees`, `review.no_review_terminal_status`, and `review.max_fix_attempts`
Default values are `allowed_assignees: [codex]`, `no_review_terminal_status: done`, and `max_fix_attempts: 2`
`ralph.sh` loads `config.yaml` via `python3` plus PyYAML before task selection and review handling
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: runtime policy is currently hardcoded in `ralph.sh`; there is no root `config.yaml`. Target config shape comes from `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` under Runtime policy and config. Root `config.yaml` is Ralph-only and separate from Backlog's own `backlog/config.yml`. Likely files: `ralph.sh`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, new `config.yaml`. Use `tools/ralph/progress.md` only as evidence of already-landed runtime work; do not update it in this task.

userStories.title: Select no-sequence work from one ordered backlog list pass
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need no-sequence selection to consume one ordered `backlog task list --sort priority --plain` result and preserve its emitted order so the runtime follows backlog ordering instead of manually iterating statuses.
acceptanceCriteria: No-sequence mode issues one `backlog task list --sort priority --plain` call to build the candidate queue
Candidate order follows the emitted backlog order exactly
Ralph loads `backlog task <id> --plain` only for candidate IDs from that ordered list
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: `ralph.sh` currently calls `backlog task list -s <status> --sort priority --plain` separately for `To Do` and optionally `Review Failed`. Target: one ordered list pass, then detail filtering. Read `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` under Selection and task lifecycle and Test Plan. Update shell fixtures so `backlog task list` without `-s` returns grouped statuses in backlog-config order. This story is about queue construction only; do not yet change candidate validity rules beyond what is needed to preserve one-pass ordering.

userStories.title: Filter candidates, revalidate before launch, and claim atomically
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need candidate validity checks and claim behavior tightened so Ralph only launches Codex for allowed tasks and claims ownership in one atomic backlog edit.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate selection accepts only allowed-status, dependency-ready tasks whose assignee is empty or listed in `selection.allowed_assignees`
Ralph reloads the chosen task immediately before Codex launch and skips invalidated non-sequence tasks
Sequence mode fails fast if a forced task is no longer valid
Claim uses one `backlog task edit` call to set assignee and status together
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: current claim flow in `ralph.sh` is intentionally two-step (`-a` then `-s`) and sequence validation only checks task existence. User explicitly changed this requirement: claim must now be atomic, and selected tasks must be revalidated just before Codex launch. Candidate rules come from `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` under Selection and task lifecycle. Relevant files: `ralph.sh`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, `config.yaml`. Keep scope to candidate filtering, pre-launch revalidation, and atomic claim only.

userStories.title: Remove legacy assignee fallback and switch JSON parsing to jq
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need runtime state read only from canonical `session_id:<id>` labels and Codex JSONL parsed with `jq` so session handling stops depending on legacy assignee encoding and brittle shell regexes.
acceptanceCriteria: Resume state comes only from `Labels: session_id:<id>` metadata
Legacy `codex@<session_id>` assignee fallback is removed from runtime and shell tests
Codex JSONL parsing uses `jq` for `thread.started`, `turn.completed`, and `turn.failed` extraction
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: `tools/ralph/progress.md` says canonical session labels already landed, but `ralph.sh`, `AGENTS.md`, and shell tests still support legacy `codex@...` fallback. This story is cleanup plus parser hardening. Read `tools/ralph/progress.md` entries for US-001, `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Workflow Implementation Audit.md`, and the plan note sections Selection and task lifecycle plus JSON handling. Update tests that currently prove legacy migration or no-`jq` behavior; after this change those tests should be removed or rewritten.

userStories.title: Replace temp-file handling with mktemp and remove iteration sleep
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need safe temporary last-message files and no fixed iteration pause so parallel or repeated runs do not collide and the loop does not sleep unnecessarily.
acceptanceCriteria: Last-message files are created with `mktemp` and cleaned up with `trap`
Fixed iteration `sleep` is removed from `ralph.sh`
Required-command checks and test fixtures include `jq`, `mktemp`, and `python3`
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Workflow Implementation Audit.md` notes timestamp-based `/tmp/ralph-last-message-<epoch>.txt` temp files and `sleep 2` between iterations. Target comes from the approved plan's JSON handling, verifier contract, and temp files section. Likely files: `ralph.sh`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, possibly `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Shell Runtime Tests.md` if fixture guidance needs updating. This is runtime hygiene; do not mix in verifier JSON or selection rewrites here.

userStories.title: Add script-side backlog task verification before completion
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need Ralph itself to verify required backlog task state after the worker returns so completion does not depend only on prompt compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: After each worker turn, Ralph verifies from `backlog task <id> --plain` that `Implementation Plan` and `Final Summary` are present and non-empty
Ralph verifies acceptance criteria and definition-of-done items are defined and checked before final status transition
Ralph verifies expected assignee and `session_id:<id>` metadata are still present before final status transition
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: `prompt-codex.md` instructs the worker to write plan/final summary and check AC/DoD, but `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Workflow Implementation Audit.md` says shell does not enforce any of this. Verification source must be backlog plain task text, not worker prose. Relevant files: `ralph.sh`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, maybe prompt expectations if wording must align. Read the plan note section Script verification and fix loop before editing.

userStories.title: Resume worker to fix script-verification gaps
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need Ralph to reopen the worker with concrete remediation instructions when script-side verification finds missing task updates, rather than failing immediately or pretending the task is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: When script verification fails, Ralph resumes the worker with a prompt that lists the missing items
Retry count is limited by `review.max_fix_attempts` from `config.yaml`
If script-verification retries are exhausted, the task moves to `Review Failed` and preserves `session_id:<id>` metadata
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story depends on `US-008` for config loading and `US-013` for script verification. Scope only script-verification remediation, not verifier remediation. Preferred repair path is same worker session when available; that detail comes from `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` under Script verification and fix loop. Test fixtures will need richer `backlog task edit` support for `--plan`, `--final-summary`, `--check-ac`, and `--check-dod` so a dumb agent should expect to extend the mock boundary.

userStories.title: Add schema-validated verifier JSON contract
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need verifier output to be machine-validated JSON instead of text markers so review outcomes can be parsed safely and deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Repo contains `verifier-output.schema.json` describing verifier output with keys `result`, `summary`, and `issues`
`prompt-verifier.md` requires JSON-only output that matches that schema
Verifier runs with `codex exec --output-schema` and Ralph parses verifier output with `jq`
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline: current `prompt-verifier.md` uses `<verification>PASS</verification>` / `<verification>FAIL</verification>` markers and `ralph.sh` scrapes those markers from last-message text. Target JSON shape is in `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` under JSON handling, verifier contract, and temp files. Relevant files: `prompt-verifier.md`, `ralph.sh`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, new `verifier-output.schema.json`. Keep worker prompt out of scope except where verifier handoff text must change.

userStories.title: Move successful work through Review and honor no-review policy
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need successful worker output to enter an explicit `Review` state before verification and to respect config-driven no-review behavior when verification is disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: When review is enabled, script-verified tasks move to `Review` before verifier runs
When review is disabled, successful tasks go to `Done` or remain in `Review` based on `review.no_review_terminal_status`
Completion checks treat `Review` as remaining work only when review is enabled
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story depends on `US-007`, `US-008`, and `US-013`. Baseline: current runtime has no `Review` status and verify-none jumps straight to `Done`. State-transition targets come from the plan note sections Script verification and fix loop and Interfaces. Relevant files: `ralph.sh`, `backlog/config.yml`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`. Keep verifier JSON parsing out of scope except as already provided by earlier stories.

userStories.title: Reopen worker on verifier failure until pass or exhaustion
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I need verifier rejection to reopen implementation work instead of immediately terminating in `Review Failed`, while still capping retries so bad tasks do not loop forever.
acceptanceCriteria: Verifier failure appends review notes and reopens the worker for fixes
Ralph reruns worker -> script verification -> `Review` -> verifier until pass or retry exhaustion
If same-session repair is impossible because session metadata is missing, Ralph starts a fresh fix session
Exhausted verifier retries move the task to `Review Failed`
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This story depends on `US-013` through `US-016`. Baseline: current verifier rejection appends notes, moves task directly to `Review Failed`, and exits. Target repair loop is defined in `basic-memory/plans/Ralph Queue-Driven Review Flow Plan.md` under Script verification and fix loop. Likely files: `ralph.sh`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, maybe prompt text if remediation messaging needs worker-specific wording. Use `tools/ralph/progress.md` only to avoid regressing already-landed session metadata behavior.

userStories.title: Align runtime tests and docs with the new contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need tests, prompts, AGENTS guidance, and Basic Memory notes to describe the new runtime contract so future subagents do not reintroduce legacy behavior or stale assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` matches single-list selection, config-driven assignee filtering, `Review` status, `jq` parsing, `mktemp` temp files, and removed legacy assignee fallback
`AGENTS.md` and relevant Basic Memory runtime notes describe the `Review` flow, `config.yaml`, verifier JSON schema contract, and no legacy fallback
`prompt-codex.md` and `prompt-verifier.md` match the remediation loop and no longer mention stale PRD or marker-based completion behavior
bash `tests/ralph-runtime.sh` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Baseline drift is visible in `AGENTS.md`, `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Shell Runtime Tests.md`, and `basic-memory/testing/Ralph Workflow Implementation Audit.md`: they still mention legacy fallback, two-step claim flow, no-`jq` fixture guards, and marker-based verifier output. This cleanup story is intentionally last so docs/tests can be aligned to the final implemented contract. Relevant files likely include `AGENTS.md`, `prompt-codex.md`, `prompt-verifier.md`, `tests/ralph-runtime.sh`, and the two runtime memory notes under `basic-memory/testing/`.
